cards.title: Nobel Laureate Jumper Joins Anthropic From DeepMind
cards.body: John Jumper, a Nobel laureate, is leaving Google DeepMind to join rival Anthropic. Jumper is one of several high-profile researchers departing DeepMind recently.

cards.title: US Government Bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models citing national security concerns. Amazon researchers allegedly discovered a way to bypass Fable 5's guardrails.

cards.title: Export Controls Fail to Stop AI Software Flow
cards.body: A 30-year history shows export controls on cybersecurity software have been ineffective. Experts question whether they'll work better with Anthropic's Mythos model.

cards.title: Anthropic Ban May Paradoxically Boost Brand Value
cards.body: The US government's ban on Anthropic's newest models has sparked debate about unintended branding effects. Cybersecurity researchers have weighed in on the national security implications.

cards.title: Reliance Weaves AI Into 500M+ User Telecom Network
cards.body: Billionaire Ambani's Reliance is integrating AI across telecom services reaching over 500 million people. The push aims to embed AI into calls, apps, and home devices.

cards.title: Allbirds CEO Launches AI Startup Solo With Big Funding
cards.body: The Allbirds CEO has launched an AI business as a sole founder with significant seed funding. The startup's next steps remain unclear despite the substantial capital.

cards.title: US Questions ASML's Top Chip Tool Presence in China
cards.body: The US alleges that ASML's advanced chip manufacturing tool may be located in China. Commercial logic suggests ASML would avoid risking its export license.

cards.title: Elastic Acquires Bug-Catching AI Startup Deductive
cards.body: Elastic has agreed to acquire Deductive AI for up to $85 million. Deductive AI uses AI to identify and resolve software bugs, founded just three years ago.

cards.title: Baseten Raises $1.5B at $13B Valuation for AI Inference
cards.body: The AI inference startup Baseten is finalizing a $1.5 billion funding round just months after its previous mega-round. The move reflects the ongoing 'inference gold rush' in AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Snap Spins Off AI Video Team as New Company Dotmo
cards.body: Snapchat maker Snap is spinning off an internal unit into new company Dotmo focused on AI video development. The move comes as Snap seeks to reduce costs from the ambitious project.

cards.title: OpenAI Lands Major Talent Ahead of Planned IPO
cards.body: OpenAI has hired Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball. The high-profile hires signal preparation for the company's upcoming IPO.

cards.title: Half of US Singles Wary of AI in Dating Apps
cards.body: About 47% of US singles view AI negatively in dating contexts, according to Match. However, many users remain open to AI assistance with profile optimization and conversation starters.

cards.title: Amazon Challenges Nvidia by Selling AI Chips Directly
cards.body: AWS is negotiating to sell its custom AI chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy estimates this represents a $50 billion opportunity for the company.

cards.title: FERC Fast-Tracks AI Data Center Power Grid Access
cards.body: Federal regulators ordered grid operators to expedite data center interconnections for faster power access. The mandate addresses connectivity but fails to solve underlying electricity supply shortages.

cards.title: Slow Tech Movement Addresses Attention Crisis Problem
cards.body: Users increasingly seek to reclaim control over their time and attention through slower technology. The trend represents a countermovement to the smartphone era's constant engagement demands.

cards.title: Disable AI Features in Google Docs Easily
cards.body: Google Docs users can turn off AI features to eliminate 'write with Gemini' pop-ups. Simple steps allow users to regain control of their document editing experience.

cards.title: Queer Eye Coach Launches Wellness App With AI Clone
cards.body: Karamo Brown debuted Kē, a wellness app featuring his AI digital clone to guide users. The app focuses on fitness, nutrition, meditation, sobriety, and personal growth.

cards.title: General Intuition Raises $300M for Embodied AI Models
cards.body: The startup General Intuition is in talks to raise $300 million at a $2 billion valuation. It trains embodied AI using Medal's dataset of 2 billion annual videos from 10 million users.

cards.title: Tech Workers Launch $5M Grassroots Political PAC
cards.body: Guardrails, a tech worker-backed PAC, is positioning itself as a populist AI movement through small donations. The $5 million effort contrasts sharply with Big Tech's $100 million political spending.

cards.title: Pixi's iOS App Transforms Text Into Interactive AR
cards.body: Pixi has launched a new iOS app that converts text messages into interactive augmented reality experiences. The platform represents the next evolution beyond stickers, GIFs, and emoji reactions.

cards.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper leaves DeepMind for Anthropic
cards.body: Jumper isn't the only big name leaving Google DeepMind. The departure signals talent migration to rival AI companies.

cards.title: US government bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5
cards.body: The government forced Anthropic to pull its newest models citing national security concerns. Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5's guardrails.

cards.title: Anthropic ban accidentally helping brand's numbers surge
cards.body: Despite government restrictions on Fable 5 and Mythos 5 releases, market metrics remain unaffected. The ban may paradoxically boost Anthropic's brand visibility.

cards.title: Deductive AI acquired by Elastic for $85 million
cards.body: The startup uses AI to catch and resolve bugs in software. Founded just three years ago, it demonstrates rapid acquisition valuations in AI tooling.

cards.title: Baseten raises $1.5B in AI inference mega-round
cards.body: The inference startup is closing a $1.5 billion round at $13 billion valuation. The funding reflects the ongoing 'inference gold rush' in AI infrastructure.

cards.title: Snap spins off AI video team into Dotmo
cards.body: Snapchat is creating a new company focused on AI video development. Current staff are leaving Snap to focus on the AI venture amid cost pressures.

cards.title: OpenAI hires Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer
cards.body: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO with major talent acquisitions. The company also hired former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball.

cards.title: Almost half of US singles negative on AI in dating
cards.body: About 47% of singles view AI negatively in dating apps. However, many users are open to AI helping with profile optimization and conversation starters.

cards.title: Amazon sells its own AI chips to competitors
cards.body: AWS is in talks to sell its AI chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy identified a $50 billion opportunity for the company.

cards.title: FERC gives AI data centers grid interconnection fast lane
cards.body: Grid operators must now prioritize data center interconnections. However, the mandate fails to address underlying electricity supply shortages.

cards.title: Google disables AI writing suggestions in Docs
cards.body: Users can now turn off pesky 'write with Gemini' pop-ups. The feature removal responds to privacy and control concerns.

cards.title: Karamo Brown launches Kē wellness app with AI clone
cards.body: The Queer Eye star created a digital AI version of himself for wellness coaching. The app covers fitness, nutrition, meditation, and personal growth.

cards.title: General Intuition raises $300M for embodied AI models
cards.body: The startup trains embodied AI using Medal's 2 billion video dataset monthly. The funding values the company at $2 billion.

cards.title: Subquadratic claims breakthrough in LLM bottleneck
cards.body: Miami-based AI startup solved a mathematical bottleneck holding back large language models for a decade. The claim emerged from stealth mode with mixed reactions.

cards.title: Brain-computer interface trials accelerate with progress
cards.body: Casey Harrell, a man with ALS, spent nearly three years using a brain implant successfully. He's now called the first 'power user' of the technology.

cards.title: Microsoft discovers new cryptocurrency-stealing backdoor
cards.body: Researchers found a lightweight backdoor malware targeting cryptocurrency assets. The discovery highlights ongoing cybersecurity threats in AI systems.
